DECLARATION OF SUSAN K. RIGGIO, ESQ. IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT REGINA ARVON, M.D.S REPLY TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100050264) FILED BY DEFENDANT ARVON M.D, REGINA
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-17-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SUTTER PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION AND SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE SUTTER DEFENDANTS' DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT'S (FAC) CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ALL OVERRULED. THE FAC ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT MR. MOUHANNA WAS IN A PHYSICIAN-PATIENT RELATIONSHIP IN THAT HIS BLOOD WAS TAKEN, GENETIC TESTING WAS PERFORMED ON IT, DEFENDANTS PROVIDED HIM GENETIC COUNSELING AND THUS HE WAS A DIRECT VICTIM. NOTHING LIKE THIS WAS ALLEGED IN THE OPINION DEFENDANTS RELY ON: BURGESS V. SUP. CT. (1992) 2 CAL.4TH 1064 (OR IN ANY OTHER CITED CASE). MOREOVER, DIFFERENT THEORIES OF NEGLIGENCE LIABILITY ARE OFTEN PLED IN SEPARATE COUNTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600=302/RU

MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-17-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT REGINA ARVON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT AVRON'S DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT'S (FAC) CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ALL OVERRULED. THE FAC ADEQUATELY ALLEGES THAT MR. AND MRS. MOUHANNA WERE IN A PHYSICIAN-PATIENT RELATIONSHIP IN THAT THEIR BLOOD WAS TAKEN, GENETIC TESTING WAS PERFORMED ON IT, DEFENDANTS PROVIDED THEM GENETIC COUNSELING AND THUS THEY WERE DIRECT VICTIMS. NOTHING LIKE THIS WAS ALLEGED IN THE OPINION AVRON RELIES ON: BURGESS V. SUP. CT. (1992) 2 CAL.4TH 1064 (OR IN ANY OTHER CITED CASE). MOREOVER, DIFFERENT THEORIES OF NEGLIGENCE LIABILITY ARE OFTEN PLED IN SEPARATE COUNTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/RU

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR OCT-23-2018 CONTINUED TO DEC-11-2018 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO FILE PROOF OF SERVICE ON DEFENDANT(S) AND OBTAIN ANSWER(S), OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AS TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT REGINA ARVON'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT AVRON'S MOTION TO STRIKE REPEATS ARGUMENTS ALREADY MADE IN ITS DEMURRER. THE ONLY ADDITIONAL ARGUMENT REFERS TO "INTENTIONAL TORT LANGUAGE" THAT DOES NOT EXIST IN THE COMPLAINT WHERE AVRON SAYS IT IS LOCATED. THE MOTION IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600 =302/RU

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Brett L. Gibbs, tel. No. 1-415-381-3104 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Alexandra C. Seibert of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-288-9800
appearing for Defendant Sutter;
Atty. Susan K. Riggio, Tel. No. 1-925-930-9090 appearing for the Defendant Dr. Arvon.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUTTER PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION and SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The Sutter defendants' demurrers to the first amended complaint's (FAC) causes of action are all OVERRULED. The FAC adequately alleges that Mr. Mouhanna was in a physician-patient relationship in that his blood was taken, genetic testing was performed on it, defendants provided him genetic counseling and thus he was a direct victim. Nothing like this was alleged in the opinion defendants rely on: Burgess v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064 (or in any other cited case). Moreover, different theories of negligence liability are often pled in separate counts. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600=302/ru

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Brett L. Gibbs, Tel. No. 1-415-381-3104 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Alexandra C. Seibert of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-288-9800
appearing for Defendant Sutter;
Atty. Susan K. Riggio, Tel. No. 1-925-930-9090 appearing for the Defendant Dr. Arvon.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT REGINA ARVON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Avron's motion to strike repeats arguments already made in its demurrer. The only additional argument refers to "intentional tort language" that does not exist in the complaint where Avron says it is located. The motion is DENIED. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Brett L. Gibbs, tel. No. 1-415-381-3104 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Alexandra C. Seibert of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-288-9800
appearing for Defendant Sutter;
Atty. Susan K. Riggio, Tel. No. 1-925-930-9090 appearing for the Defendant Dr. Arvon.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT REGINA ARVON's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Avron's demurrers to the first amended complaint's (FAC) causes of action are all OVERRULED. The FAC adequately alleges that Mr. and Mrs. Mouhanna were in a physician-patient relationship in that their blood was taken, genetic testing was performed on it, defendants provided them genetic counseling and thus they were direct victims. Nothing like this was alleged in the opinion Avron relies on: Burgess v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064 (or in any other cited case). Moreover, different theories of negligence liability are often pled in separate counts. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/ru
